title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. lindbergiana
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. lindbergiana, also known as Lady's Mantle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its rosy or pink flowers and broad, rounded leaves. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. lindbergiana has been valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and menstrual issues. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and alleviating symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats. Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. lindbergiana can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, teas, tinctures, or salves, which can be applied topically or taken internally under medical supervision.
